text: Continuous Integration, or CI, is a development practice where team members frequently merge their code changes into a shared repository. Whenever someone pushes new code, a CI pipeline automatically triggers a series of steps. This includes an automated build to compile the code and running tests to check for errors. The main goal of CI is catching issues early, before they become bigger problems. By identifying bugs and conflicts quickly, developers can fix them right away. This reduces delays and keeps the software stable. Overall, CI pipelines help teams work more efficiently and deliver higher-quality software with fewer surprises.
